root_motivation: Provide the single off-device transport boundary that closes the wm.family.ack loop for family-intents dialogs. Without this daemon every family message times out into 'I couldn't reach Joe.' Distress events bypass normal queueing via a privileged dispatch path; the transport backend (email/ntfy/webhook) is swappable via config without touching the dialog FSM.
user_persona: jsy running wintermute on a headless Arch Linux laptop; wintermute-reach runs as a systemd user service and is never invoked interactively except for test-transport and reply subcommands.

acceptance_criteria.description: wm-reach --help lists daemon, send, reply, test-transport subcommands.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac1.rs: assert --help output contains all four subcommand names

acceptance_criteria.description: The Transport trait has an email impl that, with WM_REACH_SENDMAIL pointed at a capture script, produces a message containing the family body.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac2.rs: integration test with fake sendmail capture script

acceptance_criteria.description: A published wm.family.message { to: 'joe', body: 'heating broken' } results in one transport delivery and one wm.family.ack { delivered: true, transport: 'email' } on the bus.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac3.rs: bus integration test using in-process mock bus

acceptance_criteria.description: A wm.family.distress event is delivered ahead of a wm.family.message that was queued first.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac4.rs: ordering/priority test verifying distress bypasses normal queue

acceptance_criteria.description: A transport error yields wm.family.ack { delivered: false } — not a panic, not a silent drop.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac5.rs: inject transport error, assert ack payload has delivered=false

acceptance_criteria.description: wm-reach reply 'Joe says hi' publishes a wm.family.reply { from, body } that a bus subscriber receives.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac6.rs: round-trip test via in-process mock bus

acceptance_criteria.description: ntfy and webhook backends compile behind their Cargo features and are excluded from the default build.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac7.rs: cargo build without features succeeds; cargo build --features ntfy,webhook also succeeds; grep src/ for #[cfg(feature)] guards

acceptance_criteria.description: The daemon applies the self-emitted-topic filter — does not re-consume its own wm.family.ack or wm.family.reply publishes.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac8.rs: assert self-emitted topic filter logic with mock bus

acceptance_criteria.description: systemd unit wm-reach.service installs pointing at the same bin path the install step uses (no cargo-bin-vs-local-bin drift).
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac9.rs: parse wm-reach.service ExecStart and assert it matches install_prefix/wm-reach

acceptance_criteria.description: No secret (SMTP password, ntfy token) is logged; config is read from /etc/wintermute/conf.d/, never hard-coded.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac10.rs: grep-clean test: no literal credential patterns in src/; config loader reads from file path env var

acceptance_criteria.description: cargo test green; cargo clippy clean; release gate receipts produced per autobuilder.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac11.rs: meta-test asserting prior AC pass counts and that clippy exits 0

scope: Subscribe to wm.family.message and wm.family.distress on the agorabus
Deliver messages via email (default, always compiled), ntfy (feature), webhook (feature)
Publish wm.family.ack after each delivery attempt (delivered: true/false)
Publish wm.family.reply when wm-reach reply subcommand is invoked (v1 inbound stub)
Self-emitted-topic filter to avoid re-consuming own publishes
Distress bypass-batching: distress events delivered synchronously ahead of normal queue
Config loaded from /etc/wintermute/conf.d/ — no hardcoded credentials
CLI: daemon, send, reply, test-transport subcommands
non_goals: v2 inbound reply polling (email folder polling, webhook receipt) — deferred to v2
SMS, push notifications outside ntfy, or any transport requiring phone registration
Multi-recipient delivery — v1 delivers to the single configured jsy contact only
Message persistence / retry store — if delivery fails, ack { delivered: false } and move on
Any web server or listen socket (this is a pure client-to-external-service daemon)
Encryption or signing of messages in transit beyond what the transport provides
Rate limiting or batching of normal messages (v1 delivers promptly regardless of urgency field)

additional.no_network_in_tests: All tests use fake sendmail scripts or in-process mock transports; no real SMTP or ntfy connections in cargo test
additional.no_secret_in_logs: Transport credentials must never appear in any log line, panic, or Debug output

five_whys_trace.q: Why does wintermute need an off-device transport daemon?
five_whys_trace.a: No daemon today can cross the local/off-device boundary; family messages time out into 'I couldn't reach Joe.'

five_whys_trace.q: Why does the dialog time out without this?
five_whys_trace.a: PRD-wintermute-family-intents enters FamilyPending and waits for wm.family.ack; without this daemon the ack never arrives.

five_whys_trace.q: Why not have the dialog FSM publish to an external API directly?
five_whys_trace.a: The transport choice (email/ntfy/webhook) should be swappable without touching the dialog FSM; isolation to one daemon is the right abstraction.

five_whys_trace.q: Why does distress need a privileged bypass path?
five_whys_trace.a: wm.family.distress is published on its own topic precisely so a delivery daemon can treat it as bypass-batching, highest-priority. The companion PRD established this contract; reach must honor it.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Should the agorabus dependency be a path dep from ~/wintermute/agorabus or a crates.io version?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Path dep — agorabus is not published to crates.io; use path = '~/wintermute/agorabus' (absolute path in Cargo.toml).

ambiguities_resolved.question: What session_id should the daemon use for its agorabus announce?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: wm-reach-daemon combined with the process pid, formatted as 'wm-reach-<pid>'.

ambiguities_resolved.question: How does the self-emitted-topic filter work?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Track own session_id; in the event loop, skip any ServerEvent whose 'from' field matches own session_id.
